resume:
=== Leyli Hernandez - Resume.pdf ===
MAY 24, 2009 • U.S. CITIZEN • 99-22 42ND AVE, BASEMENT, CORONA, UNITED 
STATES, 11368 • leyyhdzzz@gmail.com • (347)453-8142 

LEYLI  HERNANDEZ 

ABOUT ME 

My name is Leyli Hernandez, 17 years old. I’ve grown up and lived in Queens and am very 
passionate about helping my community in any way, anytime. I am constantly assisting in 
any event which has allowed me to be very engaging towards others whenever I get the 
opportunity. I love to stay physically active and always have something to do. I am always 
down to try new things and expand my horizons. 

VOLUNTEERING  HISTORY 

● 

● 

I spend most of the weekends or afterschool hours volunteering with Elmhurst 
Community Partnership(ECP) which is a non-profit organization that provides free 
sports clubs, social groups, community assistance, community events and other 
activities. 
I am also the Co-Chair of the Executive Board of another non-profit company I work 
with called SPEAKHIRE. SPEAKHIRE works with Middle Schools, High Schools, and 
Colleges to help students work on their leadership skills, career paths, and connects 
these students with professionals based on their career interests. I am also chair to 
the Media committee, and work with planning our monthly events to help them run 
smoother. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 SKILLS 

Team Leadership (Experienced), Community Engagement (Experienced), Problem Solving 
(Experienced), Social Media Management (Experienced), Quick Learner, Organized, Public 
Speaking (Experienced). 

EDUCATION 

HIGH SCHOOL 

40-11 28th St, Astoria, NY 11101  

Currently a Junior Student-Athlete at Academy of American Studies High 
School as I am part of the Varsity Girls Flag Football team  

LANGUAGES 

English (Native), Spanish (Highly proficient).
coach_notes: To strengthen your social-service career, focus on developing classroom management, child development, and lesson planning—these are core for roles like youth counselor or community program coordinator. Start by volunteering at local after-school programs or taking free online courses in early childhood education to build hands-on experience. Your existing skills in community engagement and leadership are a great foundation; combine them with these new competencies to make a bigger impact.

jobs.title: Cultural Site Interpreter
jobs.label: ready
jobs.why: Native Spanish and social media skills are directly applicable to cultural interpretation. Organization and planning support site operations.
jobs.gaps: Lack of formal interpretation or museum experience; may need to demonstrate cultural knowledge beyond language.

jobs.title: English and History tutor
jobs.label: ready
jobs.why: Strong match with English and History tutoring given your high school background and quick learner trait. Teamwork and communication skills are relevant.
jobs.gaps: No formal tutoring experience listed; need to emphasize any informal teaching or mentoring roles.

jobs.title: Temporary Tutor, Math
jobs.label: ready
jobs.why: Math tutoring aligns with your quick learner and teamwork skills. High school background provides foundational knowledge.
jobs.gaps: No specific math tutoring experience; need to demonstrate proficiency in math topics beyond high school level.

jobs.title: Osmosis Health Leadership Initiative (Formerly OMEF)
jobs.label: target
jobs.why: Your community events and teamwork skills align with health leadership initiatives. Native Spanish and planning are assets for diverse outreach.
jobs.gaps: No direct healthcare or leadership program experience; need to highlight transferable skills from student-athlete and event planning.

jobs.title: Educator I
jobs.label: target
jobs.why: Your teamwork and leadership from student-athlete and community events can translate to educator role. Organization and planning are useful.
jobs.gaps: No formal education or teaching experience; significant gap in curriculum development or classroom management skills.
